jokes.text: The fatwa is over! It's a new me.
jokes.setup: Larry celebrating the end of his fatwa crisis
jokes.punchline: Claims to be a 'new me' when he's obviously still the same Larry
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-awareness paradox - celebrating personal growth while demonstrating none

jokes.text: Larry claims saving someone's life by interceding at a restaurant, treating a minor social gesture as heroic
jokes.setup: Larry recounting events that led to ending a fatwa
jokes.punchline: His overblown self-importance about a trivial restaurant interaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David narcissistic reframing - turning a minor act into life-saving heroism. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: I played 36 holes of golf that day. And, uh, I fell asleep before the second act of 'Hamilton.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he fell asleep at the hottest show on Broadway
jokes.punchline: Prioritized golf over cultural phenomenon, creating exhaustion excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry priorities - golf over high culture, then making excuses. Character-specific and relatable absurdity.

jokes.text: Only you would fall asleep at the hottest show on Broadway.
jokes.punchline: Identifies Larry's unique ability to miss cultural moments
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's character but predictable response

jokes.text: Just shuck and awe. Okay, well, that's great.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'shock and awe' military term
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: He shucks all over town. I don't know what circles he shucks in. He could shuck and tell.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about shucker spreading gossip
jokes.punchline: Multiple shuck puns building to 'shuck and tell'
jokes.explanation: Escalating wordplay that captures Larry's paranoia perfectly. 'Shuck and tell' is genuinely clever.

jokes.text: Larry's paranoid worry about the Shucker overhearing his Hamilton story
jokes.setup: The Shucker overhears Larry's Hamilton confession
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate paranoia about word getting back to Lin
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic overthinking of consequences perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You've covered up that cut stone from Italy with a rug!
jokes.punchline: Horrified by Larry's decorating choices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of uncomfortable house tour dynamic

jokes.text: The wallpaper with the pirates? Are you kidding? It was the first thing I took down when I moved in.
jokes.setup: Rose asking about her decorating choices
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt dismissal of pirate wallpaper
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - brutally honest about terrible decorating. Pirate wallpaper is absurd visual detail.

jokes.text: And you know what? It happens to be my house, so--
jokes.setup: Larry getting frustrated with Rose's criticism
jokes.punchline: Asserting ownership rights mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Larry's mounting frustration with boundary-crossing behavior

jokes.text: What the fuck? I'm checking the master bedroom.
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of bedroom invasion
jokes.explanation: Escalation of home invasion absurdity - casual boundary crossing

jokes.text: Look at this poor ficus! What have you done? I left this with you in good faith to love and cherish.
jokes.punchline: Treating plant custody like child custody with wedding vows language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - using marriage vows for plant care. Perfect escalation of Rose's attachment.

jokes.text: what's the male equivalent of bush? Man bush. That, like-- so, finding his penis was like-- I'm searching for the baby bird in the-- In the nest.
jokes.punchline: Baby bird in nest metaphor for finding penis in pubic hair
jokes.explanation: Crude but creative metaphor. Shows Bridget's oversharing character but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: The Leaning Tower of Peter.
jokes.punchline: Pun on Leaning Tower of Pisa with penis name
jokes.explanation: Solid pun construction with good economy. Memorable phrase but somewhat juvenile.

jokes.text: Mrs. Shapiro sneaking back into the house during dinner
jokes.setup: Larry thought Mrs. Shapiro had left
jokes.punchline: She's been lurking and inspecting the house
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the home invasion scenario.

jokes.text: A bite on the damn nipple!
jokes.punchline: Leon's emphatic interjection about nipple biting
jokes.explanation: Leon's unexpected participation in TMI conversation. Good character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Leon's interjection about biting nipples
jokes.punchline: Leon's enthusiastic contribution to the inappropriate conversation
jokes.explanation: Leon's blunt contribution fits his character's inappropriate honesty.

jokes.text: this has been a spectacular 'shuck-cess.'
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'shuck' and 'success'
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun from character defined by wordplay. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: You know, earlier when I was talking about 'Hamilton,' what-- what you didn't hear me say was that I played, uh, 36 holes of golf that day and I was exhausted.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to do damage control with Shucker
jokes.punchline: Repeating the exact same excuse that makes him look worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - thinking the excuse makes him look better when it makes him look worse. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: You can paint it or dress it up anyway you want, but sleep is sleep. And you fell asleep.
jokes.punchline: Shucker cuts through BS with simple truth
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Larry's verbose excuse. Simple, direct, and cuts to the heart of the matter.

jokes.text: you can afford me some shucker discretion in this matter.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'sucker discretion' with 'shucker'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that fits Larry's verbose pleading style

jokes.text: It'd be a shame if Lin-Manuel Miranda ever found out that someone was falling asleep in his show.
jokes.punchline: Shucker turns to blackmail threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - service worker becomes power player. Perfect escalation of Larry's paranoia becoming reality.

jokes.text: Larry kicking everyone out immediately after dessert
jokes.punchline: Larry's abrupt hostility and 'get the hell out'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David antisocial behavior - hatred of normal social conventions.

jokes.text: I don't like all the lingering after dinner. You had your dessert. Get-- get the hell out. Come on, get out.
jokes.punchline: Larry rudely ejecting guests
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Larry behavior - social conventions are irritating obstacles. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Look at me. I'm the sheriff.
jokes.setup: Jeff putting on cowboy hat left behind
jokes.punchline: Immediately adopts sheriff persona
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag setup for hat subplot. Economical character moment.

jokes.text: She's showing me love and affection that I've never felt before. 'Cause of the hat. All 'cause of the hat.
jokes.punchline: All marital improvement attributed to cowboy hat
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise well-executed. The simplicity of the solution to marital problems is funny.

jokes.text: So I have to keep the hat on during sex. That's her thing. Yeah. I haven't had this much sex since college.
jokes.punchline: Must wear cowboy hat during sex for wife's fetish
jokes.explanation: Escalates absurd hat premise to sexual territory. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Where are the women who have a bald perversion? I wonder if there's, like, a place they all hang out. You just walk in and, you know.
jokes.punchline: Larry wants to find women with bald fetishes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-interest - immediately relates Jeff's success to his own potential benefit.

jokes.text: I was wondering, would it be possible to get two tickets for my shucker?
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for Hamilton tickets for 'his shucker'
jokes.explanation: Larry's terrible timing and priorities. Meeting Broadway star, immediately asks for favors.

jokes.text: For your shucker? Like an oyster shucker? Yeah, yeah, oyster shucker. Yeah. Fantastic shucker. So fast.
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward over-explanation of shucker quality
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Larry tries to justify bizarre request

jokes.text: Oh, 'Salmon.' Salman. Yeah, 'Salmon,' yeah.
jokes.punchline: Lin pronounces it like the fish
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation joke. Shows Lin's casual approach to serious literary figure.

jokes.text: The 'F' is for fucking awesome.
jokes.setup: Suggesting F. Murray Abraham for role
jokes.punchline: Crude interpretation of actor's middle initial
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay that doesn't elevate beyond cheap laugh

jokes.text: It's the desk. Yes. It's the power of the desk. He's up high, I'm down low. Everything he says, I say yes to. He's in the boss chair.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking why Larry agreed to everything
jokes.punchline: Larry's theory about desk-based power dynamics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry observation about power dynamics. Universal truth delivered with specific Larry neurosis. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Larry's explanation that the desk makes him subservient - 'it's the power of the desk'
jokes.setup: Jeff confused why Larry agreed to everything
jokes.punchline: Larry blames the physical power dynamic of the desk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy about office psychology and power dynamics.

jokes.text: ♪ There's a fatwa, there's a fatwa ♪ ♪ Gotta run, I think I oughta, gotta hide ♪
jokes.setup: Larry showing his musical theater songs
jokes.punchline: Terrible rhyming and rhythm in fatwa song
jokes.explanation: Larry's musical incompetence on display. Character-specific humor about his creative delusions.

jokes.text: ♪ I'm Biggie and 'Pac, who shot ya? ♪ ♪ I'm at the top of the rock and I'm hotter than guac ♪
jokes.punchline: Professional rapper flow replacing Larry's amateur attempt
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between amateur and professional. Shows Lin taking over Larry's project.

jokes.text: I wouldn't get a piano just because I wouldn't want them to move the piano into my house.
jokes.punchline: Larry avoids piano ownership to avoid watching movers work
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry logic - avoiding life experiences to avoid social discomfort. Perfectly captures his neurosis and is highly relatable.

jokes.text: If I get lonely, I'll just rent a penis.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to male prostitution as solution
jokes.explanation: Blunt delivery makes it funny but relies on shock value

jokes.text: Ah! That is a-- An unapproved 'penis.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's formal disapproval of penis discussion
jokes.explanation: Larry's bureaucratic language applied to sex talk. Good character-specific reaction.

jokes.text: Fuck me, Tex!
jokes.punchline: Susie's immediate sexual response to cowboy persona
jokes.explanation: Direct payoff to hat subplot. Economical but predictable.

jokes.text: The only downside is I gotta keep fucking my wife. Sick of it? Oh, so sick of it.
jokes.punchline: Too much sex with wife is now a problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - getting what you want becomes a burden. Dark but relatable.

jokes.text: Something funny happen in Tahoe? No. Uh-uh. Something weird? No. Nope. I think something happened in Tahoe.
jokes.setup: Jeff probing about Larry and Cheryl's Tahoe trip
jokes.punchline: Jeff's relentless interrogation despite Larry's denials
jokes.explanation: Good escalating interrogation. Jeff's persistence vs Larry's defensiveness.

jokes.text: Okay. Yeah, okay, I'll go. I'll-- I'll take the tickets. For the same day that the shucker was... Yeah. Now they're for you?
jokes.setup: Lin offering Larry tickets instead of the shucker
jokes.punchline: Larry's transparent greed in immediately accepting tickets he claimed were for someone else
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry's self-interest overriding his cover story. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: If you really wanted to find out, go ask Ted Danson.
jokes.punchline: Larry deflecting to Ted Danson
jokes.explanation: Good deflection tactic. Maintains Tahoe mystery while implicating Ted.

jokes.text: make her sign an agreement that she won't fucking talk. What do you mean, like a nondisclosure agreement? Fucking non-disclosure. Make her sign that shit.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about Bridget sharing sex stories
jokes.punchline: Leon suggests nondisclosure agreement for sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd solution that Larry will actually pursue. Leon's blunt practicality applied to ridiculous situation.

jokes.text: Leon's suggestion for a 'fuck document' - nondisclosure for sex
jokes.setup: Larry worrying about Bridget talking about their sex life
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude but practical solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept that becomes central to episode - Leon's blunt wisdom.

jokes.text: People don't sign 'fuck documents.'
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact dismissal using crude term
jokes.explanation: Larry's formal language mixed with crude terminology. Good economy and quotability.

jokes.text: Susie sprained my dick.
jokes.punchline: Physical injury from too much sex
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - explains entire situation in three words. Absurd but believable consequence.

jokes.text: She took my dick to places it wasn't meant to go. So far to the right, so far to the left. Back and forth.
jokes.punchline: Geometric description of sexual positions
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy through crude description. Extends the dick injury joke but adds little.

jokes.text: You need a dick chiropractor. Dick chiropractor? There's dick chiropractors? Yeah. Of course there are.
jokes.punchline: Leon matter-of-factly confirms existence of dick chiropractors
jokes.explanation: Leon's casual expertise about obscure medical specialties. Good absurdist logic.

jokes.text: She took the plant! Mrs. Shapiro took the plant!
jokes.punchline: Plant theft revelation
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation but necessary plot progression

jokes.text: Mrs. Shapiro stealing Larry's ficus plant
jokes.setup: Mrs. Shapiro's concern for the plant's welfare
jokes.punchline: She actually steals it for its own good
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mrs. Shapiro's boundary violations.

jokes.text: Don't give us that green thumb shit.
jokes.setup: Rose claiming to save the plant
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt dismissal of gardening excuse
jokes.explanation: Leon cutting through bullshit with characteristic directness

jokes.text: I'll tell you what. We're gonna take that dog or this plant. Oh, no, you're not taking my dog. Why not? You think I want that dog in my house?
jokes.punchline: Leon suggests taking dog, Larry horrified at prospect
jokes.explanation: Leon's extreme negotiation tactics vs Larry's practical horror. Good character contrast.

jokes.text: You know what? Let's take his hat. No, you're not taking his hat. No, take the whole goddamn head. Take the whole fucking head.
jokes.punchline: Larry escalating from hat to entire head
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy. Larry's logic: if we're stealing decorative items, go big.

jokes.text: He's a lot better-looking than you. Oh, is that so? Mm-hmm. I'm an Uber four, okay? Well, Myron was an Uber five.
jokes.punchline: Rating attractiveness using Uber rating system
jokes.explanation: Clever modern reference for rating attractiveness. Larry's self-awareness about his looks.

jokes.text: Well, what do you mean? That's-- that's like a-- that's like a bad horse trade.
jokes.punchline: Shucker's folksy comparison to horse trading
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - shucker using Western idioms

jokes.text: For $2,500. Take it or leave it. You're quite a prick, aren't you? It's the Shucker's way.
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of being called a prick as professional identity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Shucker owns his opportunism as professional identity

jokes.text: I just like to get an erection, put it in a vagina. That's it? That's about it, yeah. That's your biggest fantasy?
jokes.setup: Bridget asking about Larry's sexual desires
jokes.punchline: Larry's mechanically simple description of sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry sexual philosophy - reducing romance to basic mechanics. Hilariously unromantic yet honest.

jokes.text: This is a relationship nondisclosure agreement? Yeah. Is this a joke? No, no.
jokes.punchline: Bridget's disbelief at legal document for relationship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to Leon's suggestion. Larry actually creating legal document for sex is peak absurdity.

jokes.text: Sign this and you'll have the wildest sex you ever imagined! Orgasms galore! You won't regret it!
jokes.punchline: Larry desperately selling sex contract like infomercial
jokes.explanation: Larry treating sex like business transaction with sales pitch. Excellent cringe escalation.

jokes.text: You're missing out on Tahoe Larry!
jokes.punchline: References mysterious Tahoe incident as selling point
jokes.explanation: Larry using mysterious Tahoe incident as sexual credential. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: The Shucker appearing on Judge Judy as 'Larry Long Balls'
jokes.setup: Judge Judy episode about Larry vs. Mrs. Shapiro
jokes.punchline: Bridget recognizing the Shucker as her sex story subject
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that connects multiple plot threads - the sex stories, Judge Judy appearance, and the Shucker.

jokes.text: Yes, that's Larry Long Balls.
jokes.setup: Bridget identifying Larry on Judge Judy
jokes.punchline: Reveals Larry's embarrassing nickname from her stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Larry's paranoia about being talked about. Embarrassing nickname revealed publicly.

jokes.text: This plant is like a person. Certain parts of the year, that plant turns darker, like myself. When I go to Florida, I turn a little darker, a shade darker. Most of the time I carry myself in a mocha tone, but there are some parts of the year when I'm more mahogany.
jokes.punchline: Extended comparison between plant and his own skin tone variations
jokes.explanation: Leon's elaborate tangent about skin tone variations as plant expertise. Perfectly absurd courtroom testimony.

jokes.text: Let's get finished with the various tones of Mr. Black and let's move on to something else.
jokes.punchline: Judge Judy's dry dismissal of irrelevant testimony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Judge Judy response - cutting through nonsense with deadpan efficiency

jokes.text: Anybody want a cough drop? You want one, Judge? Judge Sheindlin? You want a cough drop?
jokes.punchline: Larry inappropriately offering cough drops during trial
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate social behavior in formal setting. Perfect courtroom disruption.

jokes.text: That is not filtered water. Don't drink it. Don't drink it. Put it down.
jokes.punchline: Larry's urgent warning about water quality
jokes.explanation: Larry's health paranoia disrupting court proceedings. Perfectly inappropriate concern.

jokes.text: Are we no better than the-- The beasts in the field? And by beasts, I'm not talking about the-- the chicken. I'm talking about the oxen and the-- the donkey...with its slack-jawed stare
jokes.punchline: Elaborate biblical metaphor with specific donkey description
jokes.explanation: Larry's pretentious moral outrage with unnecessary animal specificity. Classic verbose Larry rant.

jokes.text: Are we no better than the-- The beasts in the field? And by beasts, I'm not talking about the-- the chicken. I'm talking about the oxen and the-- the donkey... with its slack-jawed stare
jokes.setup: Larry's outrage at losing the Judge Judy case
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate animal hierarchy speech with specific donkey insults
jokes.explanation: Good Larry rant escalation. The animal hierarchy and specific donkey description is absurd and character-appropriate, if a bit long.

jokes.text: I'm not a shucker. I enjoy clams. I do. I like oysters, but I don't shuck. I know a shucker, but that doesn't mean that the shucker's gonna be here. I'm here, not the shucker.
jokes.punchline: Obsessive clarification about not being the shucker
jokes.explanation: Larry's verbose anxiety about mistaken identity. Too repetitive but character-accurate.

jokes.text: Larry falling asleep during Hamilton performance
jokes.setup: Larry taking pain pills and attending Hamilton
jokes.punchline: Falls asleep again at Hamilton, next to Lin's wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect full-circle callback. Larry's worst nightmare realized - sleeping at Hamilton next to creator's wife. Excellent visual comedy.
